name: browser_tool
description:
Browser automation tool — navigate websites, click buttons, fill forms, make reservations, and complete multi-step web workflows. Uses a real browser (Chromium) so it works with JavaScript-heavy sites like OpenTable, Resy, etc.

Actions:
- navigate: Go to a URL (returns page content + interactive elements)
- click: Click a button, link, or element (by CSS selector or visible text)
- type: Type text into an input field (search boxes, forms, etc.)
- screenshot: Take a screenshot and get a description of what's on screen
- get_elements: List all clickable buttons, links, inputs, and dropdowns
- select: Choose an option from a dropdown menu
- scroll: Scroll the page up or down
- back: Go back to the previous page
- get_text: Get all visible text on the page
- close: Close the browser session when done

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Always call 'close' when you're done with the browser to free resources!
⚠️ CONTEXT WARNING: navigate and get_elements return element lists that can be large. Use screenshot for a quick visual overview instead.

Typical workflow:
1. navigate to URL → see what's on the page
2. click/type/select to interact with elements
3. screenshot if you need to see the visual state
4. close when done

url.description: URL to navigate to (for 'navigate' action). Can include https:// or not.

selector.description: CSS selector or Playwright text selector for the target element. Examples: '#submit-btn', '[name="email"]', 'text="Sign In"', '.reservation-button'. Use selectors from get_elements results.

text.description: For 'type' action: the text to type into the input field. For 'click' action: visible text of the element to click (alternative to selector).

value.description: For 'select' action: the option value or visible text to select from a dropdown.

amount.description: For 'scroll' action: number of scroll steps (1-10, each step ~300px)

description.description: What you're trying to accomplish on this page. Helps the vision model provide better screenshot analysis.
